translation: And the LORD desired to crush him; he made him sick. If he makes his soul an offering for guilt, he will see offspring and prolong his days; and the LORD’s pleasure will prosper in his hand.

alternate_translations.translation: It was the LORD’s will to break him and to cause him suffering; if he offers his life as a guilt offering, he will see descendants, he will live a long life, and the LORD’s purpose will be accomplished through him.
alternate_translations.notes: This follows the common interpretive rendering of the Hebrew’s conditional phrasing (“if…”) as a statement about what will result from the guilt-offering; “accomplished through him” reflects בְּיָדֹו (“in his hand”/“through his agency”).

alternate_translations.translation: But the LORD took delight in crushing him, in making him suffer; when he sets his soul as a guilt offering, he shall see seed, shall prolong days, and the LORD’s desire shall succeed by his hand.
alternate_translations.notes: More literal in diction: “took delight” for חָפֵץ, and “sets his soul” for תָּשִׂים נַפְשֹׁו, while preserving יַד/“hand” as the sphere where the LORD’s desire succeeds.

notes: This verse is part of the “Servant” passage (commonly Isaiah 53). חָפֵץ (“desired/pleased”) expresses divine will or pleasure. תָּשִׂים אָשָׁם (“make … a guilt/offering”) refers to sacrificial guilt imagery (often rendered “offering for sin” in English). וְחֵפֶץ יְהוָה בְּיָדֹו יִצְלָח means the LORD’s will/pleasure will succeed “in his hand” (i.e., through the servant’s action/agency).
